content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 9, 1993

Steve A. Sloan
Chairman
San Diego Police Officers Association
619 Kettner Boulevard
San Diego, California 92101-6718

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-223

Dear Mr. Sloan:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
the San Diego Police Officers Association concerning the
permissible use of his campaign funds under the Political Reform
Act ("the Act").1/

QUESTION

May the San Diego Police Officers Association use campaign
funds from its political action committee to host a dinner for
delegates and congressional attendees of the National Association
of Police Organizations' annual conference?

CONCLUSION

The association may use committee funds to pay for the dinner
for the delegates and congressional attendees of the conference so
long as the event is directly related to a political purpose.

FACTS

The San Diego Police Officers Association, in conjunction
with the National Association of Police Organizations (NAPO), will
be hosting NAPO's 1993 Annual Conference in San Diego. The
purpose of the conference is to discuss the national agenda for

1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-93-223
Page 2


law enforcement issues with NAPO, which serves as the national
lobbyist for the San Diego Police Officers Association.

As part of the conference, the San Diego Police Officers
Association will be hosting a dinner for the conference delegates
and congressional guests at Sea World to allow the delegates to
meet and confer with the out-of-state congressional guests. All
of the delegates and congressional guests will be invited to
attend the dinner.

ANALYSIS

The general rule of the personal use law is that absent an
express provision pertaining to the expenditure, an expenditure of
campaign funds must be reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose. Expenditures which confer a
substantial personal benefit on the candidate or controlling
member must be directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. (Section 89512; Section 89512.5.)

Furthermore, Section 89513(f)(1) provides that an expenditure
of campaign funds for personal gifts is permitted only when the
gift is directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. The Act defines the term "gift" to mean a
payment to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value
is not received. (Section 89028.) Therefore, if you use campaign
funds to pay for a meal for the invited guests to the conference
you will be making a gift to the attendees.

For an expenditure of the committee for a personal gift to be
permissible, there must be a direct relationship between the
expenditure for the dinner and a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. (Olander Advice Letter, No. A-92-211 and
Braly Advice Letter, No. A-90-596.) Your inquiry, therefore,
requires a determination whether the costs of the dinner are
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose.

The association is a co-sponsor of the conference. The
purpose of the conference is to educate and inform delegates and
congressional guests on the national agenda for law enforcement
issues. According to our telephone conversation of June 5, 1993,
the association is hosting the dinner to provide an opportunity
for the delegates and congressional guests to meet and convey the
concerns of the committee and its members. Therefore, we conclude
that provided the dinner is a conference event, the dinner
provided to the attendees as part of the conference would be a
gift which is directly related to a political purpose.

Our File No. A-93-223
Page 3


     I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Jeff Marschner
                                        General Counsel

                                        [handwritten signature]
                                        By:  Luisa Menchaca
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

QUESTION: May the San Diego Police Officers Association use campaign
funds from its political action committee to host a dinner for
delegates and congressional attendees of the National Association
of Police Organizations’
annual conference?

CONCLUSION: The association may use committee funds to pay for the dinner
for the delegates and congressional attendees of the conference so
long as the event is directly related to a political purpose.
